page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-8-lssd is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 64 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled via a single 402 GB local NVMe SSD, while no complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations fall within the average range. However, memory bandwidth exhibits a significant bottleneck under heavy uncached workloads; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 565.66 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths drop to weak and poor categories, respectively. LLM inference speeds are average for small models but drop to weak for medium-sized model prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a dense memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with fast local NVMe storage, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive uncached memory write throughput. The server is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, text processing pipelines, and general-purpose database operations where local high-speed scratch space is beneficial.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-8-lssd is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database performance. However, uncached large-block memory write bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10 percent. With an 8 GB RAM per vCPU ratio and fast local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that require local scratch space. It is highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching systems, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-8-lssd is an x86_64 server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-8-lssd features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 402 GB local NVMe SSD for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64 GB RAM providing an 8 GB per vCPU ratio
Includes a bundled 402 GB local NVMe SSD for fast storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
